mnemonics: Bayes' Brain: Use 'Bayes' Bill' to imagine a bill (probability calculation) that helps you update your beliefs (posterior) based on new evidence (likelihood) and prior knowledge (prior).
keyPoints: Probability-based learning uses statistical methods to make predictions or decisions based on data.
Bayes' Theorem is a fundamental rule that updates the probability for a hypothesis as more evidence becomes available.
A Naive Bayes classifier applies Bayes' theorem with the 'naive' assumption of independence between every pair of features.
commonMistakes: Confusing prior probability P(A) with posterior probability P(A|B). The prior is the initial belief, the posterior is the updated belief after seeing evidence.
Misapplying the 'naive' assumption of feature independence in Naive Bayes, which can hurt performance when features are highly correlated.
Forgetting that probabilities must sum to 1 over all possible outcomes, leading to incorrect normalization in calculations.
